clean_body: We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | "" Peter, Pro-choice Democratic women can take back the Senate in 2018. To do it, we need to re-elect our 10 Senate women and help two candidates flip red seats. But we can't take anything for granted. Each and every one of our Senate women needs our help to overcome crowded primary elections, toss-up races, or swing state politics. "" All of our Senate candidates are facing tough races: Tammy Baldwin's (WI) seat is a top target for the GOP this cycle. A long list of Republicans are vying to challenge her in a state President Trump won by a slim margin in 2016, the first time a Republican presidential candidate won the state in 32 years. Maria Cantwell (WA) was one of the first women to defeat a sitting elected senator in a general election. She's up for another challenge this year as she faces off with her Republican opponent in a state known for close races. We need Dianne Feinstein's (CA) experience and grit in the Senate, where she is the ranking member of the powerful Senate Judiciary Committee and holds Trump's judicial nominees accountable. Kirsten Gillibrand (NY) won her election for her first full term in the Senate by record-breaking margins. She can do it again with our help. There's a crowded primary of Republicans vying to challenge Mazie Hirono (HI) -- she's the only immigrant in the Senate and has tirelessly fought back against Trump's anti-immigrant agenda. Amy Klobuchar (MN) is facing a crowded primary in a state Hillary Clinton won by only two points and where Republicans have been making gains at the state level. Trump-endorsed Republican Josh Hawley is challenging Claire McCaskill (MO) in a toss-up race in a state Trump won by nearly 20 points. And the GOP is spending huge sums of money to help him defeat her. In her run for the House in 2016, Jacky Rosen (NV) beat the odds and flipped an open red seat despite the millions the GOP spent against her. She can do it again this year as she runs in a toss-up race to replace Sen. Dean Heller -- the only Senate Republican facing re-election in a state Clinton won. Kyrsten Sinema (AZ) is running in a toss-up race to fill the open red seat being vacated by Sen. Jeff Flake. The Republican primary has been called a "free-for-all," attracting extremists like Sheriff Joe Arpaio, who's known for gross human rights violations, and Kelli Ward, who received support from Trump and Steve Bannon. Tina Smith (MN) is running to return to the Senate for the first time. Holding this seat is critical to taking back the Senate, and Republicans are doing everything they can to defeat Tina in a state Clinton won by only two points. John James, who Trump called a "star," is challenging Debbie Stabenow (MI) in a state the president won by less than one point. The first woman to represent her state in the Senate, Elizabeth Warren (MA) is fighting to keep her seat as Republicans, emboldened by unexpected gains in 2016, step up to challenge her. All 12 of these women are key to flipping the Senate. Donate $3 to help elect pro-choice Democratic women to the Senate: "" Thanks, Lucinda Guinn Vice President of Campaigns, EMILY's List "" | EMILY's List is a community of over five million members that helps elect pro-choice Democratic women to office. | Paid for by EMILY's List "" and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 1800 M Street NW, Suite 375N, Washington, DC 20036 Thank you for being a supporter of EMILY's List. name: Amanda Eubanks
subject: Checking in before the weekend
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress ***
body:
I'll be blunt. I received some bad news. And now, we have to raise $4,000 by midnight. I'll ask you to contribute to this Rapid Response Fund, but first let me walk you through what's going on.

Let me say, our ground game has been incredible. We've knocked on thousands of doors, made phone calls, and registered voters throughout the district. Kathy has put thousands of miles on her car driving across the district talking with voters about what keeps them up at night. This is the largest grassroots effort North Carolina's 13th District has ever seen. And thanks to people like you, that hard work of our team has made this one of the most competitive races in the country.

But our momentum is in jeopardy. Here's why:

The Heritage Action for America, an extreme, conservative Washington special interest group, is investing $400,000 to make sure Kathy isn't elected.

Now, I need you to donate $18 to our Rapid Response Fund.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kme180809?refcode=kme180809_m&recurring=true&amount=18

Make no mistake -- they're targeting Kathy for a reason. The group specifically mentioned that they are targeting races with promising Democratic candidates.

And this investment came shortly after a single-digit poll was released.

They know that as a Congresswoman, Kathy will always represent the families of North Carolina. It would be a huge problem for Heritage Action for America and these other extreme Washington special interest groups if Kathy was elected and they lost their bought-and-sold ally, Rep. Ted Budd.

Will you donate to fill our Rapid Response Fund? We are aiming to reach $4,000 by midnight tonight.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kme180809?refcode=kme180809_m&recurring=true&amount=18

In a post-Citizens United world, I don't know how many more "bad news" messages I will have to send. But what I do know is that Kathy won't win without your support.

Rush in a contribution by using this link: www.kathymanning2018.com/rapidresponse

Thanks,
Tori

Tori Taylor
Campaign Manager
Kathy Manning for Congress

*** Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress ***

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
Click here to unsubscribe: https://manning.bsd.net/unsubscribe

www.kathymanning2018.com

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: Looking Out for Hoosiers
body:
As our team here at your Indiana Republican Party continues to talk with Hoosiers across the state (including at our State Fair booth -- stop by... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us11.campaign-archive.com/?e=1acd9bb3d6&u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1bb564dacc)
---
| |
---
|
---
|
---
| **_Looking Out for Hoosiers_** As our team here at your Indiana Republican Party continues to talk with Hoosiers across the state (including at our State Fair booth), we're hearing a consistent theme: Hoosiers want elected officials who will stand with us, and not with out-of-touch Democrats who want to recklessly tax us more, spend more, and make our communities less secure.

And we've built up a strong track record of delivering on those expectations here in Indiana. And it's all because of the great work of our state's leaders -- like **Governor Eric Holcomb** and **Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch** \-- and the great work of our dream team 2018 statewide ticket of **Secretary of State Connie Lawson, Treasurer Kelly Mitchell** and **Auditor Tera Klutz**.

For example, if you took a look around the Statehouse, you'll find...
* **Connie Lawson** working hard to make it easier for Hoosiers to vote, but harder for them to cheat. You'd see her alerting Hoosiers to ways to protect themselves against investment fraud. And you'd see her making it easier for small business to work with the state of Indiana through a one-stop-shop online portal called INBiz.
* **Kelly Mitchell** working to make it easier for Hoosiers to save money – including helping Hoosiers save $4.5 billion for college through the CollegeChoice 529 Plan (and lowering fees!) -- and launching a new savings plan – INvestABLE – which helps Hoosiers with disabilities save for life’s expenses.
* **Tera Klutz** making our state government more open and accountable – including launching a big overhaul to the state’s transparency portal – and keeping Indiana the fiscal envy of the nation by maintaining our responsible budget reserves.

It's the same leadership we're seeing from our excellent Republican delegation in Congress, and it's the leadership we'll see when we send in **Mike Braun** to be our next U.S. Senator!

But first, it's critical that we send home Democrat Joe Donnelly – someone who’s voted against lowering our federal taxes, voted to keep the status quo in health care by maintaining the broken Obamacare law, and was a proponent of Barack Obama’s dangerous Iran Nuclear Deal, which would have allowed Iran to expand its uranium enrichment program just 10 years into the program.

This November, we must re-elect**Connie Lawson, Kelly Mitchell** and **Tera Klutz** – and we must replace the out-of-touch Democrat Donnelly with the real Hoosier conservative in the race – **Mike Braun.**

-Kyle

_**Photo:** Our Republican U.S. Senate candidate, Mike Braun, stopped by the Indiana Republican Party booth at the State Fair this week. Swing by the booth, which is located just north of the covered bridge near the Free Stage, and sign our Mike Braun banner with your county. The county with the most signatures at the end of the State Fair wins! _

| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
|
---

| **_Next Level Jobs on the Road_**
This week, **Governor Eric Holcomb** celebrated the one-year anniversary of [Next Level Jobs](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=cfb310891b&e=1acd9bb3d6) \-- a big initiative aimed at helping Hoosiers build their career skills -- by going on the road and meeting with the Hoosiers who make it all possible!

Through Next Level Jobs, Hoosiers can earn free job training in high-wage, high-demand fields. And that's while employers can receive up to $50,000 in reimbursements for eligible workforce training.

There's so many great examples of Hoosiers taking their careers to the Next Level, like:
* The more than 1,300 Hoosiers across the state who have gone through culinary or nurse aid apprenticeship programs through Trilogy Healthcare. **Governor Holcomb** visited their Springhurst Health Campus location in Greenfield.
* The high school students and other Hoosiers who have taken advantage of the apprenticeship programs, high school internships, second chance and mentoring programs at Gaylor Electric in Noblesville.
* The students at Madison Consolidated High School who are taking classes at Ivy Tech Madison.
* The 66 women at the Madison Correctional Facility who graduated from a manufacturing certificate program through Ivy Tech (with **Governor Holcomb** there cheering them on).
* The students at Perry Central High School in southern Indiana who work for Commodore Manufacturing, a manufacturing facility on-site at their school where they apply the skills they're learning in the classroom.

If you'd like to take your career to the Next Level like these Hoosiers, [learn more about free training available](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2127a7df05&e=1acd9bb3d6) through Next Level Jobs!

| **_Mike Braun the Doer!_**
This week, **Mike Braun** is hitting the airwaves with his latest ad, "Doer!"

In the fun new ad, we see a clear contrast between the two U.S. Senate candidates. You have **Mike Braun** , who's spent his life building a business headquartered in his hometown of Jasper, Indiana, providing good wages and health care to his employees. And then you have Democrat Joe Donnelly, who has built a career voting with his Democrat bosses Nancy Pelosi and Chuck Schumer.

[Watch this latest ad](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d2a0b98db8&e=1acd9bb3d6)...and you'll see why in this race, Mike Braun is the doer!
